rationale: Both correctly diagnose the issue as a run-level misconfiguration (default core_model loads dummy weights) rather than a real bug, and both give the same correct fix (--run-level=full_model) and disposition (closed as invalid), matching the thread. X claims the full_model pytest marker itself 'gates' execution behind the run-level, which is inconsistent with the observed log (the test actually ran and produced output rather than being skipped/errored), and X pads its citations with placeholder ellipsis comment URLs rather than real evidence. Y correctly notes the marker is only a categorization filter independent of the --run-level flag, which better explains why the test executed instead of being skipped, and backs its explanation with a more granular chain of file/line citations plus practical completeness (video path, GPU memory split, forward-looking fixture-guard suggestion).
